title: Using flow cytometry for mitochondrial assays
author: Monteiro, Davanzo, et al.

steps.title: MitoTracker green and mitotracker red staining for functionality assay
steps.instruction: MitoTracker Green details:¬≠Stock solution (reconstituted in DMSO): 1¬†mM¬≠Working concentration: 20‚Äď200¬†nM

steps.title: MitoTracker green and mitotracker red staining for functionality assay
steps.instruction: MitoTracker Red details:-Stock solution (reconstituted in DMSO): 1¬†mM-Working concentration: 25‚Äď500¬†nM

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: First, we calculate how many samples will be stained with the complete mix. We use 50¬†¬ĶL of probes mix per well. We use PBS¬†+¬†2% FCS to dilute the probes and stain the cells.

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: For this experiment, we used the following panel to stain the macrophages (Table 1).Table 1Flow cytometry panel for mitochondrial parameters in macrophages.Table 1Target/ProbeFluorochromeClone/ Cat. numberLive/DeadV500 - Zombie Aqua‚ĄĘ Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 367/526LIVE/DEAD‚ĄĘ Fixable Aqua Dead Cell Stain Kit, for 405¬†nm excitation ThermoFisher Scientific Catalog number: L34957Anti-mouse F4/80Brilliant Violet 421‚ĄĘ Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 405/21Clone: BM8 Isotype: Rat IgG2a, őļ Biolegend Catalog number: 123,137MitoTracker‚ďá GreenFITC ‚Äď Green Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 490/516MitoTracker‚ĄĘ Green FM - Special Packaging Invitrogen‚ĄĘ Catalog number: M7514MitoTracker‚ďá RedPE ‚Äď Red Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 579/599MitoTracker‚ĄĘ Red CMXRos - Special Packaging Invitrogen‚ĄĘ Catalog number: M7512

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: For macrophage surface staining and Live/ Dead staining, we diluted the anti-F4/80 antibody 1/200 and the Live/Dead probe was diluted 1/500.

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: From the stock solution of MitoTracker Green, we suggest diluting 10000x and keep a final concentration of 100¬†nM. This can be achieved by diluting the stock solution 1/100 in PBS and then diluting 1/100 again into the final mix.

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: For MitoTracker Red, we dilute the stock solution once (1/100) in PBS and then again 1/50 into the final mix for a final concentration of 50¬†nM.

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: We also separated one well for our unstained control and two wells for our MitoTracker Green and MitoTracker Red FMOs (fluorescence minus one). Each FMO control contained all the dyes, except either MitoTracker Green or Red.

steps.title: Preparation of probes
steps.instruction: Pre-warm the mix (37‚ÄĮ¬įC).

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Wash the cells with cold PBS 1x. We used a 48-well culture plate with 3.5¬†√ó¬†105 cells.

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: If you are working with adherent cells, gently scrape the cells from the plate with a cell scraper.

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Transfer the cells to a 96-well round-bottom plate. Centrifuge for 5¬†min at 1500¬†rpm and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in pre-warmed antibody and probes mix (50¬†¬ĶL per well). Add 50¬ĶL of each FMO mix to their respective wells and add 50¬†¬ĶL of PBS¬†+¬†2% FCS to the unstained well. Incubate for 15¬†min at 37‚ÄĮ¬įC.

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Once the staining time is over, add 100¬†uL of warm PBS¬†+¬†2% FCS to wash the mix off the cells and centrifuge again (5¬†min at 1500¬†rpm and discard the supernatant).

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Finally, resuspend the cells in PBS¬†+¬†2% FCS for acquisition in the flow cytometer.

steps.title: Cell staining
steps.instruction: Since the MitoTracker Green dye is not fixable, the samples should be acquired as soon as the staining is over to minimize cell death.

steps.title: MitoSOX staining: production of mitochondrial superoxide
steps.instruction: MitoSOX Red details:-Stock solution (reconstituted in DMSO): 5¬†mM-Working concentration: 5¬†¬ĶM

steps.title: Preparation of mix
steps.instruction: Since MitoTracker Red and MitoSOX have very similar excitation and emission wavelengths, these two probes cannot be used together. Therefore, we stained MitoSOX separately, using the panel from Table 2.Table 2Flow cytometry panel for evaluation of mitochondrial ROS in BMDMs.Table 2Target/ProbeFluorochromeClone/ Cat. numberLive/Dead CalceinBlue Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 495/515BD Calcein Blue AM Fluorescent Dye FisherScientific Catalog number: BDB564060Anti-mouse CD11bPE-Cy7 Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 496/785Clone: M1/70 Isotype: Rat IgG2b, őļ Biolegend Catalog number: 101,216MitoSOX‚ĄĘPE - Red Excitation‚ĀĄEmission (nm): 510/580MitoSOX‚ĄĘ Red Mitochondrial Superoxide Indicator Invitrogen‚ĄĘ Catalog number: M36008

steps.title: Preparation of mix
steps.instruction: For macrophage surface staining and Live/ Dead staining, we diluted the anti-CD11b antibody 1/200 and the Live/Dead probe was diluted 1/500 in PBS¬†+¬†2% FCS.

steps.title: Preparation of mix
steps.instruction: From the stock solution of MitoSOX Red, we suggest diluting 1/10 in PBS and then 1/100 into the final mix. This results in a final working concentration of 1/1000, or 5¬†¬ĶM.

steps.title: Preparation of mix
steps.instruction: Again, we determine one well for our unstained control and 1 well for MitoSOX Red FMO. The FMO consists of 50¬†¬ĶL of all dyes, except MitoSOX Red.

steps.title: Preparation of mix
steps.instruction: Pre-warm the mix (37‚ÄĮ¬įC) and stain the cells as described above in the cell staining guide.

steps.title: Analysis of mitochondrial ROS production by pro-inflammatory macrophages
steps.instruction: During oxidative phosphorylation part of the electrons flowing through the ETC ‚Äúleaks‚ÄĚ and rapidly interacts with molecular oxygen to form superoxide anion, which is the most important ROS in the mitochondria [19]. MitoSox is a live-cell permeant dye with the cationic triphenylphosphonium substituent, the positive charge of this molecule allows the uptake of the probe by actively respiring mitochondria. Once inside of the mitochondria, another constituent of MitoSOX called dihydroethidium can be oxidized by superoxide generating the 2-hydroxyethidium, which exhibits a fluorescence excitation peak at ~400¬†nm and emission detection at ~590¬†nm [20].

steps.title: Analysis of mitochondrial ROS production by pro-inflammatory macrophages
steps.instruction: Here, we stained the macrophages using the panel described on Table 2. For our living cells selection, we used calcein, which is a cell permeant dye that is hydrolysed by living cells and becomes fluorescent. Therefore, we select the Live/Dead+ events. Previous studies have described that M1 macrophages produce more mitochondrial ROS and that is important for the killing of pathogens [21]. Taking this into consideration, we stimulated BMDMs with IFN-ő≥ (20¬†ng/mL)¬†+¬†LPS (100¬†ng/mL) to induce an M1 phenotype (classically activated, pro-inflammatory macrophages). The M0 macrophages were given PBS. We selected macrophages from the gate of living cells (Live/Dead+ and CD11b+) (Fig. 3A). We used a representative histogram to show that the M1 macrophages have higher MitoSOX MFI than M0 macrophages after 6¬†h of IFN-ő≥ and LPS treatment (Fig. 3B). This result is quantified in a bar graph with statistical analysis (n¬†=¬†6) (Fig. 3C).Fig. 3Analysis of mitochondrial superoxide production by M1 macrophages. BMDMs. were treated with 20¬†ng/m0L IFN- ő≥¬†+¬†100¬†ng/mL LPS for 6¬†h to induce an M1 phenotype. Macrophages were stained with: macrophage surface marker CD11b, a Live/Dead fluorescent dye and the mitochondrial probe MitoSOX Red. A. Gating strategy to select living macrophages, stained CD11b+Live/Dead+. B. Representative MFI of mitochondrial superoxide production by macrophages treated with either vehicle (M0) or IFN- ő≥+LPS (M1). C. Quantified MFI of mitochondrial ROS production by M0 and M1 macrophages. Error bars are mean¬†¬Ī¬†SEM *p¬†<¬†0.05, (n¬†=¬†6).Fig 3
